scope: GPU=per-frame GPU time (o32=kernel pipeline, i16=H2D+compute+D2H); one-time ctx+build(~90ms)+file I/O excluded. CPU=wall-clock.
note: INT16 slower on GPU (paired-int32 emulation, no native INT64, 2 serial estimator phases) = silicon-honest fixed-point, not GPU-speed. o32 ~30-40x faster than sRGB DL baselines (approx: GPU kernel vs end-to-end).
cpu_gpu_note: @1080p wall-clock warm. GALOSH GPU=kernel pipeline; others end-to-end. CPU=1 core. GALOSH only method on both platforms; B2U requires GPU.

r32_cpu_1080p_phase_breakdown_s.note: r32 CPU is the bit-exact reference (correctness-first, not speed-optimized); heavy per-pixel phases in paired-int32 emulation = ~10-20x FP32. Fast INT path = GPU i16 (73.8ms). INT16 NOT in the speed table.

isp_note: GPU/CPU INT speed = FP-host emulation artifact, NOT ISP speed. op-count model (MAC/px / ISP MAC-rate) is the host-independent ISP-native metric. 0.5 TMAC/s ISP -> 1080p 73fps; 1 TMAC/s -> 4K 36fps. ~197x lighter than DnCNN. See isp_throughput_model.md

mac_per_pixel_comparison.note: GALOSH measured (-DGALOSH_OPCOUNT); NLM exact S^2*P^2 (S=23,P=5); BM3D est block-match (N1=8,Ns=39,step3,2-stage); B2U measured via Conv2d hooks (UNet wf48 d5): one forward ~72k, actual 17-forward re-visible inference ~1.22M MAC/full-px. GALOSH lightest (21-360x < B2U) AND best LPIPS among blind. DnCNN-B excluded (sRGB, not raw).
